stat_of_week: 75 - Hiroshima Radiation's pitching staff racked up 75 strikeouts across 69.2 IP, tying Abbotsford-Colby Twins' league-record mark from week 2 while also posting a 3.10 ERA and league-high 6 QS en route to an 8-2 demolition
thriller_note: A dead-even tie where General Hospital dominated RBI and strikeouts but got crushed by Buttville's 2.25 ERA, leaving both teams without a clean win in a week where General Hospital's expected wins of 1.54 made the tie a painful near-miss that could haunt their standings.
key_storyline: Hiroshima Radiation announced themselves as a genuine title contender with a dominant 8-2 thrashing of Canberra Wombats, posting 75 K, 6 QS, and a 3.10 ERA while Taj Bradley (17 K, 1.59 ERA) and Nolan McLean (15 K, 2.38 ERA) looked like one of the league's most dangerous one-two pitching punches. Meanwhile Canberra had a monster offensive week led by Gunnar Henderson's 5 HR and Jacob deGrom's return to filth (15 K, 1.64 ERA), yet still got blown out 8-2 — a brutal result that exposes just how deep Hiroshima truly is. After sitting at the bottom of the power rankings just weeks ago, Hiroshima's back-to-back strong weeks signal a real surge up the standings.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers posted a league-low 34 strikeouts, no quality starts, and a brutal 5.53 ERA from their pitching staff, yet somehow walked away with a 6-3 win over Riverside Roughnecks thanks to their offense outperforming expectations in counting categories.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs posted 62 strikeouts, a solid 4.30 ERA, 5 QS, and 7 NSVH — competitive numbers that should have been more rewarded — but managed only a 5-4 loss to Albuquerque Bombers despite a luck delta of -0.60, the worst in the league this week.
records_broken: North Correa's 11 stolen bases in Week 3 tied (and per the records data, was already set as the record), so no new records were broken this week.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Three straight top-tier category weeks, 2.11 ERA this week, Matt Olson (3 HR, 8 RBI) and Drake Baldwin anchoring a balanced roster that just won't quit | same
2. Hiroshima Radiation | 8-2 blowout win with 75 K, 6 QS, league-best pitching depth — Taj Bradley and McLean are an elite duo, bats quietly contributing | up
3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker exploded for 5 HR and 6 RBI, Logan Gilbert was surgical, and this roster has multiple weeks of elite production — still a top-3 threat | same
4. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood (3 HR, 8 RBI, .636 OBP) and Willson Contreras (3 HR, 11 RBI) both went nuclear; 6-3 win is comfortable; real contender | same
5. General Hospital | Manny Machado (3 HR, 11 RBI) and Cam Schlittler (15 K) are difference-makers but a 4-4 tie against Buttville when expected to win 1.54 is a frustrating step back | down
6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 8-2 despite Gunnar Henderson, Yordan Alvarez, and deGrom all having strong weeks — still a deep roster but badly outgunned by Hiroshima | down
7. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Ildemaro Vargas (.600 OBP) was a waiver gem, Masyn Winn adds real upside, and their pitching metrics remain solid despite the 5-4 loss | up
8. North Correa | Shohei Ohtani (4 HR, .529 OBP) and Chris Sale (22 K) are a star-studded core but the rest of the roster couldn't back them up enough in a 5-3 loss | same
9. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Kris Bubic's 0.43 WHIP and Brandon Woodruff returning are encouraging signs, but the offense still can't generate enough counting stats to compete consistently | same
10. Albuquerque Bombers | Four waiver additions including Josh Jung and Braxton Garrett show aggressive management; Kyle Harrison and Landen Roupp both pitched well in the 5-4 win | up
11. Riverside Roughnecks | A 6-3 win over Benchwarmers is a win, but zero quality starts and a 5.04 ERA from the rotation is a ceiling-limiting problem that won't go away on its own | same
12. Buttville Skidmarks | A 4-4 tie feels like a win for this roster — MacKenzie Gore's 0.60 WHIP and Elly De La Cruz's 4 SB are real weapons, but the rotation depth is dangerously thin | up

13. Canberra Wombats | Cal Raleigh, Henderson, and deGrom gave them one of the more impressive individual-player weeks in the league and they still got blown out — roster balance is the fatal flaw | down
14. Benchwarmers | Michael King (12 K, 3.00 ERA) continues to be wasted — no QS, 34 team strikeouts, and a 5.53 ERA mean even a 6-3 win can't hide how fragile this roster really is | same
waiver_highlights: Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A | Added Ildemaro Vargas (1B,2B), Scott Barlow (RP), Dustin May (SP), Masyn Winn (SS) | Four adds in one week, with Vargas posting a .600 OBP immediately and Winn adding SS eligibility and SB upside — aggressive and impactful roster construction

Albuquerque Bombers | B | Added Keaton Winn, Braxton Garrett, Josh Jung, Noelvi Marte | Four adds targeting rotation depth and position scarcity; Jung especially fills a real need at 3B; shows a manager fighting hard to stay competitive

General Hospital | B | Added Kumar Rocker (SP); dropped Jack Leiter, Janson Junk, Cristian Javier | Clearing out the rotation deadwood in one move — Rocker is a high-upside bet and the three drops were all liabilities eating roster space

North Correa | C | Added Austin Wells (C), Brayan Rocchio (2B,SS); dropped J.T. Realmuto (C), Brooks Lee | Realmuto drop is notable given his name value, but Wells has been the better producer this season — a sensible if unspectacular refresh

Riverside Roughnecks | C | Added Victor Vodnik (RP); dropped Mark Vientos (1B,3B) | Losing Vientos hurts positional flexibility and offensive upside; Vodnik adds bullpen depth but the tradeoff feels like a step sideways at best
